info.title: Capra Rove Sensor Interface
info.description:
Robot sensor API with UDP transport and HTTP documentation.

## Discovery

`GET /discover` lists all sensors with their endpoints and UDP ports.

## Per-Sensor Endpoints

Each sensor has its own routes:
- `GET /{sensor_id}/info` - schema, commands, UDP packet format
- `GET /{sensor_id}/data` - current data snapshot
- `POST /{sensor_id}/command` - send a command
- `POST /{sensor_id}/estop` - emergency stop (supported drivers only)

## UDP Protocol

Packets: `| version (1B) | msg_type (1B) | seq_num (2B LE) | JSON payload |`

### Data Subscription
Send **Subscribe (0x01)** to the sensor's data port. The robot pushes **Data (0x03)** packets to your address continuously. Send **Unsubscribe (0x02)** to stop.

### Commands
- **REST sensors**: Send **Command (0x10)**, get **CommandAck (0x11)**.
- **Stream sensors** (CAN watchdog): Send **StreamStart (0x12)** once, robot re-sends to hardware at interval. **StreamStop (0x13)** to cancel.

get.description: Returns every registered sensor with its ID, name, UDP ports, command mode, and HTTP endpoint paths.

post.description:
Start a calibration sequence on **Kinova Gen2 6DOF (Custom Spherical)**.

**Body**: `{"type": "full" | "motor" | "encoder_index" | "encoder_offset"}`

| type | Axis State | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `full` | 3 | Full calibration (motor + encoder) |
| `motor` | 4 | Motor calibration only |
| `encoder_index` | 6 | Encoder index search |
| `encoder_offset` | 7 | Encoder offset calibration |

The drive must be in **Idle** state before calibrating. The sequence runs asynchronously — poll `/data` to watch `axis_state` return to Idle (1).

Send a command to **Kinova Gen2 6DOF (Custom Spherical)**.

Mode: Stream (100ms watchdog)
UDP command port: 5003

The JSON body is the same payload used in UDP Command (0x10) packets.

get.description: List the commands accepted by **Kinova Gen2 6DOF (Custom Spherical)** as a `name → {type, description, unit}` map. Same fields as `command_schema` in `/info`, presented as a lookup table.

Read current data from **Kinova Gen2 6DOF (Custom Spherical)**.

This mirrors what you'd get via UDP subscription on port 5002.

Read a single endpoint from **Kinova Gen2 6DOF (Custom Spherical)** by path. Returns `{path, value, type}`.

Paths come from `GET /kinova_arm/endpoints`.

List the readable endpoints exposed by **Kinova Gen2 6DOF (Custom Spherical)**. For most drivers this is the data-schema field set; for ODrive it is the loaded `flat_endpoints.json` map. Use `GET /kinova_arm/endpoint/{path}` to read a single value.

Full schema and UDP protocol details for **Kinova Gen2 6DOF (Custom Spherical)**.

Mode: Stream (100ms watchdog)
Data UDP port: 5002
Command UDP port: 5003

200.description: Sensor info with schemas and UDP protocol

get.description: Returns the directory tree under `LOG_DIR` as `{date → {hour → {sensors[], inputs}}}`. Use `GET /logs/file/{path}` with the relative path to download a specific CSV.

Download a CSV file from the log tree. `path` is a forward-slash-separated path relative to `LOG_DIR`, e.g. `2026-05-02/14/kinova_arm.csv` or `2026-05-02/14/inputs.csv`. Path traversal (`..`) is rejected.

post.description:
Upload the ODrive `flat_endpoints.json` file to enable config read/write on all nodes.

**How to get the file** (on your dev machine where odrivetool is installed):
```
python3 -c "import odrive, os; print(os.path.dirname(odrive.__file__))"
```Then find `flat_endpoints.json` in that directory.

**Upload via curl:**
```
curl -X POST http://raspberrypi.local:8080/odrive/endpoints \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  --data-binary @flat_endpoints.json
```

Or paste the file contents directly in the request body below.

Start a calibration sequence on **ODrive Node 31**.

**Body**: `{"type": "full" | "motor" | "encoder_index" | "encoder_offset"}`

| type | Axis State | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `full` | 3 | Full calibration (motor + encoder) |
| `motor` | 4 | Motor calibration only |
| `encoder_index` | 6 | Encoder index search |
| `encoder_offset` | 7 | Encoder offset calibration |

The drive must be in **Idle** state before calibrating. The sequence runs asynchronously — poll `/data` to watch `axis_state` return to Idle (1).

Send a command to **ODrive Node 31**.

Mode: Stream (250ms watchdog)
UDP command port: 5009

The JSON body is the same payload used in UDP Command (0x10) packets.

get.description: List the commands accepted by **ODrive Node 31** as a `name → {type, description, unit}` map. Same fields as `command_schema` in `/info`, presented as a lookup table.

Read calibration/configuration parameters from **ODrive Node 31** via CAN SDO.

Returns float and integer fields read from the drive firmware using `flat_endpoints.json`. Requires `ODRIVE_ENDPOINTS` env var at startup.

post.description:
Write configuration parameters to **ODrive Node 31** via CAN SDO.

**Supported keys** (all optional):
- `phase_resistance` (float, Ω)
- `phase_inductance` (float, H)
- `current_lim` (float, A)
- `vel_limit` (float, rev/s)
- `pos_gain` (float)
- `vel_gain` (float)
- `vel_integrator_gain` (float)
- `pole_pairs` (int)
- `cpr` (int, counts per revolution)

get.description:
Read current data from **ODrive Node 31**.

This mirrors what you'd get via UDP subscription on port 5008.

Read a single endpoint from **ODrive Node 31** by path. Returns `{path, value, type}`.

Paths come from `GET /odrive_31/endpoints`.

post.description: Write a single endpoint on **ODrive Node 31** by path. Body must be `{"value": <number|bool>}` matching the endpoint type.

get.description: List the readable endpoints exposed by **ODrive Node 31**. For most drivers this is the data-schema field set; for ODrive it is the loaded `flat_endpoints.json` map. Use `GET /odrive_31/endpoint/{path}` to read a single value.

post.description:
Send an **immediate emergency stop** to **ODrive Node 31**.

Disarms the motor with `ESTOP_REQUESTED`. No payload required.

get.description:
Full schema and UDP protocol details for **ODrive Node 31**.

Mode: Stream (250ms watchdog)
Data UDP port: 5008
Command UDP port: 5009

200.description: Sensor info with schemas and UDP protocol

The JSON body is the same payload used in UDP Command (0x10) packets.

200.description: Sensor info with schemas and UDP protocol

oneOf.description: Request/response: client sends a single command, server replies once.

Streaming: client sends Command packets continuously at `interval_ms`.
Each packet is processed as it arrives — the server does not loop or
re-send anything. Typical use: CAN control loops sending ODrive setpoints.

interval_ms.description: Expected client send interval in milliseconds.

CommandMode.description: Whether commands are one-shot or arrive as a continuous stream.

FieldDescriptor.description: Describes one field in a data or command schema (for OpenAPI/Scalar docs).

tags.name: logs
tags.description: Time-series CSV logs. Sensor snapshots and commands are written to LOG_DIR/<YYYY-MM-DD>/<HH>/<sensor>.csv and inputs.csv, split hourly.
